iframe_sandbox_verification.mounted_via: JS workaround (UI button-path blocked by 422); confirmed sandbox attribute is set verbatim to 'allow-scripts' by buildSandboxAttribute() in frontend/src/components/artifact-viewer.ts (line 16-18). Iframe rendered the stub HTML including dynamic timestamp script execution.

partial_reasons: Page 4 (build) Step 4e: frontend/backend contract mismatch — frontend spec-result.ts line 71 POSTs { session_id } but backend generate.rs GenerateRequest requires from_capsule: bool. UI button-click returns HTTP 422 'missing field `from_capsule`'. This breaks the click-through user flow even though the underlying spec→generate substrate works correctly when the proper payload is sent. RECOMMENDED FIX: either (a) add #[serde(default)] to GenerateRequest.from_capsule, or (b) update spec-result.ts to send from_capsule: false.

Page 2 (agents) is fixture-mode: only 1 of 10 canonical AgentRole tokens (MarketMaker) appears. Agent_001 deployed by setup script is not present in the /api/agents fixture response. Known W1-era limitation, not new regression.
notes: Phase 7 §6a v2 — re-oriented around spec→generate flow per architect 2026-05-18 ratification of 方案 A. Lean substrate (lean_market chaintape advancement) deliberately out of scope; W5/W6 substrate (POST /api/spec/submit → spec.md, POST /api/generate → artifacts, <tos-artifact-viewer> iframe sandbox security) is what was being proven here. Iframe sandbox security holds end-to-end. The 422 click-through bug is a genuine frontend↔backend contract gap that the auditor should treat as a real PARTIAL finding.
